# fix(dataflow): redact classified fields on Express mutation returns (#490)

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `0a9da432a7b5` — fix(dataflow): redact classified fields on Express mutation returns (#490)

**Body**:

DataFlowExpress mutation paths echoed the row back to the caller as a
plain dict/row — leaking PII / HIGHLY_CONFIDENTIAL field values on every
create(), update(), upsert(), upsert_advanced(), bulk_create(), and
bulk_upsert() response regardless of caller clearance. The read path
(read() / list() / find_one()) already applied masking via
_apply_classification_mask_record; the leak was in the mutation return
surface.

Fix applies the same read-path redaction on every mutation return:

- create(): _apply_classification_mask_record on the CreateNode result.
- update(): read-back via self.read() (already masked) — portable
  across dialects since SQLite UpdateNode returns metadata-only while
  PostgreSQL UPDATE ... RETURNING returns the row. Load-bearing
  delegation comment added per dataflow-classification.md MUST Rule 2.
- upsert(): read-back via self.read() using data["id"], with
  _apply_classification_mask_record on the legacy "record" extraction
  path as a fallback.
- upsert_advanced(): mask both the nested "record" dict and any
  top-level classified column echoed on the envelope.
- bulk_create(): _apply_classification_mask_rows on the list / records
  / items return shape.
- bulk_upsert(): _apply_classification_mask_rows on the "records" list;
  the scalar counts (created / updated / total) are exempt per
  dataflow-classification.md MUST Rule 4.
- bulk_update(): pinned delegation comment — redaction via self.update()
  on each row; inlining SELECT + row_to_dict here would reopen the leak.

DataFlowExpressSync is a thin _run_sync(_express.method(...)) wrapper;
the async fix covers both surfaces. The BulkOperations class in
features/bulk.py returns only scalar counts — no record rows in return
shapes — so no change needed there.

Verified:
- 6 regression tests in tests/regression/test_issue_490_mutation_return_redaction.py
  (SQLite-backed, always-on guardrail against pytest collection).
- 9 Tier 2 integration tests in
  tests/integration/security/test_classification_mutation_return.py
  against real PostgreSQL, exercising PII clearance round-trips,
  bulk_update redaction-via-delegation, and bulk_upsert record-list
  redaction.
- 10 #491 Tier 2 event-payload tests still green (same file no
  longer referenced).
- 21 pre-existing Express integration tests still green.

Pre-commit hook bypass (core.hooksPath=/dev/null): pytest-check hook
uses absolute worktree paths; same workaround as 36060c98, per
rules/git.md § Pre-Commit Hook Workarounds.

Closes #490
Cross-SDK: kailash-rs commit 2e9dbf94 (same bug pattern, EATP D6).
Related: #491 (companion event-payload classification hash).
Related: #493 (3 pre-existing security-suite failures on main,
unrelated to this fix — filed separately for dedicated session).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
